cards.title: Coregulation: How Self-Soothing Actually Develops
cards.body: A baby's ability to self-soothe depends on age, distress level, and temperament. Parental help isn't a bad habit—it's a developmental necessity.

cards.title: A Cure for Physical Punishment
cards.body: Physical punishment harms children in 15+ documented ways. Teaching both parents and children to name emotions—anger, fear, distress—transforms feelings into words rather than violence.

cards.title: Caregiving as Ways to Bond With Stepchildren
cards.body: Caregiving is care plus giving—the nurturing and supporting part of childrearing. Stepparents should focus on bonding through active care and support.

cards.title: Preserving Parental Legacies for Grieving Children
cards.body: Grieving children need comfort to preserve a parent's legacy as they navigate life without them. This support is essential during a difficult transition.

cards.title: When Children Are Expected to Be the Adults
cards.body: What happens when children are forced to suppress their pain and become adults prematurely? This dynamic shifts childhood development and emotional wellbeing.

cards.title: Family Estrangement: Effects on Wellbeing
cards.body: Estrangement is a continuum of reducing contact, not binary. Support plays an important role whether families seek reconciliation or not.

cards.title: Childcare Affordability: A Priority for Families
cards.body: Billions are being spent to help families with childcare, but profiteers may be denying families the full benefit. New measures aim to stop hidden charges.

cards.title: Young People Leaving Care Get New Support
cards.body: A new scheme for young people leaving care tackles what was once a cliff-edge moment for this vulnerable group. The program embraces youth with support and love.
